response.justification: Bogdan has relevant geospatial and GIS experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrates distributed systems knowledge from high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby. However, the role's core requirement — deep, production-grade C++ or Rust development — is nearly absent from his profile. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month contract at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, with no mention of Rust. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript, and his geospatial work targets NGO data collection rather than routing tile generation, pipeline latency optimization, or navigation guidance systems. The skill gap in the primary language and domain specificity is too significant for a mid-senior IC role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G talk
Demonstrated distributed systems and high-throughput experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency pipelines)
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across roles
concerns: C++ experience is shallow and dated (6 months in 2014–2015); no Rust experience mentioned anywhere
Geospatial work is focused on data collection and visualization for NGOs, not routing tile generation, map freshness pipelines, or navigation systems
Current and recent roles are entirely TypeScript/Node.js full-stack web work, showing no trajectory toward systems-level or C++ development
